steps.title: Spot the context
steps.content: The plane is accelerating down the runway for takeoff. The word "gather" describes what the plane does with speed in that moment. A replacement word must describe physically building up or increasing velocity.

steps.title: Eliminate and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Gain': means to acquire or increase — "the plane begins to gain speed" naturally describes acceleration on a runway. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Doubt': means to be uncertain about — speed is not something a plane can be uncertain of; this makes no physical sense. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Welcome': means to receive gladly — a plane cannot welcome speed; this is not a physical action. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Force': means to compel by strength — "force speed" is not a natural phrase for building velocity and does not fit the context. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
